name: Crazy Egg Heatmap & Analytics API
what_it_does: Web analytics platform focused on visual heatmaps, scroll maps, session recordings, and A/B testing — with API for managing snapshots and accessing analytics data, helping teams understand where users click and how they scroll on pages.
use_cases: Accessing heatmap and click data programmatically for UX analysis automation
Creating page snapshots via API to track UI changes over time
Retrieving A/B test results for automated reporting and decision-making
Integrating Crazy Egg data with analytics dashboards via API
Triggering snapshot captures after deployments for automated UX monitoring
not_for: Real-time error monitoring and alerting (use Sentry or Datadog)
Deep product analytics with event tracking at Mixpanel/Amplitude depth
best_when: A team needs visual heatmap analysis with A/B testing to optimize conversion rates, with API access for automation of snapshot management.
avoid_when: You need real-time error tracking, backend monitoring, or deep behavioral analytics beyond visual heatmaps.

auth.notes: API key from Crazy Egg account settings. Bearer token authentication. Account-scoped access. Limited API surface — primarily for snapshot management. Data export requires higher-tier plans.

pricing.paid_tiers: Basic from $29/month; Standard from $49; Plus from $99; Pro from $249
pricing.notes: Credit card required for trial. One of the original heatmap tools — founded 2006. A/B testing included in all plans. Price scales with page views tracked. Strong brand recognition in CRO space.

performance.rate_limits: Limited API — rate limits not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Very limited API — primarily for snapshot creation and management. Most analytics data accessed via UI dashboard. API documentation minimal. Visual data (heatmaps) not directly queryable as structured data.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Snapshot creation creates new snapshots. Read operations for snapshot metadata are idempotent. Analytics data queries are idempotent reads.
known_agent_gotchas: Very limited API surface — most features are UI-only and not accessible via API
Heatmap image data returned as URLs, not structured data — limited for agent processing
Credit card required for trial — higher barrier to testing
GDPR consent management required — recordings blocked by cookie banners

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. GDPR compliant. US data residency. Established product with long track record. Limited API reduces attack surface but also reduces utility.
